results.title: Reducing Cost of LLM Agents with Trajectory Reduction
results.snippet: While LLM agents demonstrate promising effectiveness, the high computational cost of input tokens due to ever-growing trajectories remains a significant efficiency concern. An inference-time trajectory reduction approach can reduce computational costs.

results.title: LLM-Agents-Papers: A repo lists papers related to LLM based agent
results.snippet: A repository listing papers related to LLM-based agents.

results.title: Improving the Efficiency of LLM Agent Systems through Trajectory Reduction
results.snippet: The high computational cost of input tokens due to ever-growing trajectories. Trajectory reduction approach can reduce computational costs for LLM agents.

results.title: LEGOMem: Modular Procedural Memory for Multi-agent LLM
results.snippet: Modular procedural memory framework for multi-agent LLM systems.

results.title: Decentralized Trajectory Optimization for Multi-Agent Ergodic Exploration
results.snippet: Decentralized trajectory optimization for multi-agent ergodic exploration in robotics. Multi-agent systems perform ergodic coverage of an environment.

results.title: AgentDiagnose: An Open Toolkit for Diagnosing LLM Agent Trajectories
results.snippet: LLM agents produce rich, multi-step trajectories that interleave observations, internal reasoning, and tool actions. Most evaluation pipelines focus solely on end-task success, leaving the agent's decision-making process opaque. AgentDiagnose is an open-source, modular framework for diagnosing agent trajectories.

results.title: TrajTune: Trajectory-Based Prompt Optimization for LLM Agents
results.snippet: TrajTune is a trajectory-aware prompt optimization framework. It captures structured execution traces, computes fine-grained error metrics, and compares them against adaptive thresholds for LLM agents.

raw_responses.query: Birkhoff ergodic theorem reinforcement learning policy collapse invariant measure

results.title: The Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem with Applications
results.snippet: Birkhoff ergodic theorem: for a measure-preserving transformation that is ergodic and invariant measure, the time average equals the space average almost everywhere.

results.title: A Note on the Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem
results.snippet: The classical Birkhoff ergodic theorem states that for an ergodic Markov process the limiting behavior of the time average of a function converges to the space average with respect to the invariant measure.

results.title: Ergodic theory - Wikipedia
results.snippet: Ergodic theory is a branch of mathematics that studies statistical properties of deterministic dynamical systems. It is concerned with the relationship between time average and space average of a function with respect to an invariant measure.

results.title: A Note on the Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem
results.snippet: A note on Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem under assumption that the process admits an invariant probability measure and its marginal distributions converge to the invariant measure.

results.title: Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem - an overview
results.snippet: Overview of the Birkhoff ergodic theorem in measure theory and dynamical systems.

raw_responses.query: ergodic theory neural network policy mode transition agent

results.title: Model-Agnostic Solutions for Deep Reinforcement Learning in Non-Ergodic Contexts
results.snippet: In non-ergodic contexts, the optimal policy cannot be accurately inferred from expected values alone. By explicitly enforcing path dependence, an agent can transition to the optimal policy and align with the Kelly criterion. Increasing model complexity alone is insufficient when training agents in non-ergodic contexts.

results.title: Deep neural networks from the perspective of ergodic theory
results.snippet: By adopting ergodic theory considerations on top of viewing the network as the time evolution of a dynamical system, with each layer corresponding to a temporal instance. Viewing stochastic gradient descent (SGD) as a Markov process on a compact weight space allows one to invoke classical ergodic theorems and obtain invariant measures that describe the distribution of network parameters over time.

results.title: Non-ergodicity in reinforcement learning: robustness via ergodic transformations
results.snippet: Non-ergodicity in reinforcement learning addressed via ergodic transformations of the reward signal. Robustness of policies learned in non-ergodic environments.

results.title: Reinforcement learning with non-ergodic reward increments: robustness via ergodicity transformations
results.snippet: Transforming the time series of collected rewards into one with ergodic increments enables learning robust policies by optimizing the long-term reward for individual agents rather than the average across infinitely many trajectories.

results.title: Description of the Training Process of Neural Networks via Ergodic Theorem: Ghost nodes
results.snippet: Description of the training process of neural networks via ergodic theorem, introducing ghost nodes.

results.title: An ergodicity perspective on reinforcement learning
results.snippet: Ergodicity Economics blog post on applying ergodic perspective to reinforcement learning, distinguishing ensemble average from time average for policy decisions.

results.title: Ergodic Theory and Agentic Ideation in AI
results.snippet: Discussion of how ergodic theory connects to agentic ideation in AI systems.

results.title: Fast Ergodic Search With Kernel Functions
results.snippet: Fast ergodic search using kernel functions for robotic exploration with information-theoretic coverage.

results.title: IterResearch: Rethinking Long-Horizon Agents with Interaction Scaling
results.snippet: Long-horizon agents evaluated on benchmarks including Humanity's Last Exam, BrowseComp, deep research, multi-step tool use, web navigation, complex reasoning, long-horizon information-seeking.

results.title: Plan-and-Act: Improving Planning of Agents for Long-Horizon Tasks
results.snippet: Plan-and-Act framework incorporates explicit planning into LLM-based agents with a Planner model that generates high-level plans and an Executor model that translates plans into actions.

results.title: UltraHorizon: Benchmarking Agent Capabilities in Ultra Long-Horizon Scenarios
results.snippet: UltraHorizon measures foundational capabilities essential for complex real-world challenges, using exploration as a unifying task across distinct environments. Trajectories average 200k+ tokens and 400+ tool calls.

results.title: AMA-Bench: Evaluating Long-Horizon Memory for Agentic Applications
results.snippet: Autonomous agents require long-horizon reasoning and experience reuse to complete tasks. AMA-Bench evaluates memory for agent applications across multiple domains.

results.title: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage? Diagnosing Where and Why Agentic Systems Break
results.snippet: Diagnostic study of where and why agentic systems break on long-horizon tasks.
